statement.focus_question: Is it true that, for every bipartite graph $G$, there exists some $\alpha\in [1,2)$ and $c>0$ such that\[\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\sim cn^\alpha?
statement.normalized: Is it true that, for every bipartite graph $G$, there exists some $\alpha\in [1,2)$ and $c>0$ such that\[\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\sim cn^\alpha?\]Must $\alpha$ be rational?
statement.original: Is it true that, for every bipartite graph $G$, there exists some $\alpha\in [1,2)$ and $c>0$ such that\[\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\sim cn^\alpha?\]Must $\alpha$ be rational?
